summary: Combines two inputs by preserving existing structure where possible and recursively merging compatible collection values.
signature_intent: Accepts two arbitrary values plus optional configuration and returns a merged value.
behavior: Returns the first input unchanged when the second input is falsy.
Appends a primitive second input to an array first input.
Adds a primitive second input as a truthy key on an object first input when allowed by safety rules.
Returns a two-item array when neither input can directly receive a primitive value.
When the second input is an object and the first input is not, returns an array containing the first input followed by the second input, expanding arrays.
Merges arrays by index, recursively merging object pairs at matching indexes and appending conflicting primitive or incompatible values.
Merges object keys by recursively combining existing keys and copying new keys.
edge_cases: Falsy second inputs are ignored entirely.
Array indexes missing from the first input are assigned directly from the second input.
Merging an array with a non-array object returns an object-like result rather than preserving array behavior.
Primitive keys that match protected prototype properties are skipped unless configuration permits them.
forbidden_details: No variable names, repository names, source paths, or low-level implementation quirks are included.
